statement: All phrase heads are right-final (postpositions not prepositions)
Declarative asertiva = main clause with ASER modopersonal
Interrogativa total = INTE modopersonal on predicate (no question word)
Interrogativa parcial = WH word + INTE modopersonal on predicate
notes: Question word is a separate constituent

statement: Negative interrogative compatible with all clause types
Subject of estative = base of predication (entity); subject of dynamic = agent or patient
OD marked by -aʼs (sg) / -txiʼs (pl); omitted when both OD and OI present (only OI marked)
OD also dropped when contextually obvious
Locative arguments take INE -te / -ka / -su / -khe according to body-axis

spanish_gloss: corté la carne con el cuchillo
esta mañana estuve en el cabildo

statement: Focalised constituent receives the displaced modopersonal+distance
spanish_gloss: fue el gato quien mordió a Carlos
notes: Distance+modop moved off predicate to focus

statement: Object focus: the OBJ-marked NP carries the modopersonal
spanish_gloss: fue a Carlos a quien el gato mordió
fue en la casa donde el gato mordió a Carlos
fue el sábado cuando el gato mordió a Carlos

statement: Voz activa is default; agent = subject
Voz media: patient promoted to subject via prefix yaʼ-
Same prefix yaʼ- = true reflexive depending on context
Compound = two simple clauses joined by yuxtaposition (no morpheme)

spanish_gloss: yo me fui pero tú descansaste

statement: Subordinate temporal with same subject = SS suffix on the subordinated verb
Subordinate with different subject = DS suffix

notes: Specific morpheme forms vary by aspect

statement: Relative clause = NOM nominaliser -sa on subordinated verb
spanish_gloss: la mujer que es bonita cocinó

statement: Nominal relative can function as subject / OD / OI / attribute
spanish_gloss: la comida de los que trabajan es buena

statement: Predicativity is universal: every lexeme accepts MPN and may predicate
notes: N V VN Q all predicate directly

statement: Word-sentence: a single predicative word constitutes a full clause
Distance morphemes -ki- (D1) / -ku- (D2) / -ne- (D3) appear in the predicate complex
Possessive 3sg adds -ji benefactive to disambiguate from demonstrative
Reciprocal RECI uses prefix yaʼ- + plural agreement
notes: Reflexive and reciprocal share the prefix; number disambiguates
